body.u: I left school in May 1978 at the age of 16. I had a few C.S.E which were average then. I tried a test for a job which I passed. I now began a job at London, which I got £35 a week but most of it went. I had to pay my mum £10 a week plus £15 on train fares. After about six months I began to get fed up out of £35 a week I was left with £10. I began to think about getting a flat in London as this would save money. Finally I got a flat for £10 per week. After a few years life began to get boring I was living alone in a flat in London. I met this girl and six months later we got married we brought a house in Middlesex and about five years later I had enough money to buy a car I brought a chryslerChrysler Alpine which would last the rest of our lives. Altogether we had 3 children 2 girls and 1 boy we saw them grow up. I still had the same job until I retired. The money was not so good but I enjoyed the work. Looking back it was not so bad even though some times we did struggle financisallyfinancially but I got a good pension I now have six grandchildren and I am happily retired.
